summary: Nigerian author writing in pidgin/non-standard English. Known for The Palm-Wine Drinkard and My Life in the Bush of Ghosts. Drew on Yoruba folklore.

works.name: The Palm-Wine Drinkard
works.description: Novel about a man searching for his dead palm-wine tapster in the Dead's Town.

clues.clue: Protagonist searches for dead palm-wine tapster in Dead's Town; encounters various supernatural beings; mother proclaims 'this is what hatred did'
examples: the main character's mother proclaims 'this is what hatred did'

clues.clue: The 'Invisible and Invincible Pawn' reattaches heads of decapitated soldiers
examples: the 'Invisible and Invincible Pawn' reattaches the heads of decapitated soldiers

clues.clue: Nigerian author; wrote in pidgin/non-standard English; drew on Yoruba folklore
examples: Nigerian author writing in non-standard English

comprehensive_summary: Amos Tutuola (1920-1997) was a Nigerian author who wrote in pidgin/non-standard English, drawing heavily on Yoruba folklore. The Palm-Wine Drinkard follows a protagonist who searches for his dead palm-wine tapster in the Dead's Town, encountering supernatural beings along the way. His mother proclaims 'this is what hatred did.' My Life in the Bush of Ghosts is his other major work.

cards.front: Novel: protagonist searches for his dead palm-wine tapster in Dead's Town
Novel: encounters supernatural beings drawn from Yoruba folklore
